items.name: cors
items.description: CORS middleware.  Supports exact origins, wildcard `'*'`, arrays of allowed origins, and suffix matching with a leading dot (e.g. `'.example.com'` matches `sub.example.com`).

options.notes:
Allowed origin(s).  Use `'*'` for any,
an array for a whitelist, or a string
starting with `'.'` for suffix matching.
Headers the browser is allowed to read.

items.example:
app.use(cors());                                  // allow all origins
  app.use(cors({ origin: 'https://example.com' })); // single origin
  app.use(cors({                                     // fine-grained
      origin: ['https://my.example.com', '.example.com'],
      credentials: true,
      maxAge: 86400,
  }));

items.name: compress
items.description: Response compression middleware using Node's built-in `zlib`. Supports gzip, deflate, and brotli (Node >= 11.7). Zero external dependencies.

options.notes: Minimum body size in bytes to compress.
`(req, res) => boolean` — return false to skip compression.

items.name: helmet
items.description: Security headers middleware. Sets common security-related HTTP response headers to help protect against well-known web vulnerabilities (XSS, clickjacking, MIME sniffing, etc.). Inspired by the `helmet` npm package but zero-dependency.

options.notes: CSP directive object or `false` to disable.

items.name: static
items.description: Static file-serving middleware with MIME detection, directory index files, extension fallbacks, dotfile policies, caching, custom header hooks, and HTTP/2 server push for linked assets.

options.notes: Default file for directory requests, or `false` to disable.
`(res, filePath) => void` hook to set custom headers.

HTTP/2 server push. Array of paths
(relative to root) to push when serving HTML files, or a function `(filePath) => string[]`.

params.notes: Root directory to serve files from.

items.example:
app.use(serveStatic('public'));                            // serve ./public
  app.use(serveStatic('dist', { maxAge: 86400000 }));       // 1-day cache
  app.use(serveStatic('assets', { extensions: ['html'] })); // .html fallback

items.name: rateLimit
items.description: In-memory rate-limiting middleware. Limits requests per IP address within a fixed time window.

options.notes: Maximum requests per window per IP.
(req) => boolean; return true to skip rate limiting.
(req, res) => void; custom handler for rate-limited requests.

items.name: timeout
items.description: Request timeout middleware. Automatically sends a 408 response if the handler doesn't respond within the configured time limit. Helps prevent Slowloris-style attacks and hung requests.

options.notes: HTTP status code for timeout responses.

items.example:
app.use(timeout(5000)); // 5 second timeout
  app.use(timeout(10000, { message: 'Too slow' }));

items.name: requestId
items.description: Request ID middleware. Assigns a unique identifier to each incoming request for tracing and debugging. Sets the ID on both the request object and as a response header.

options.notes: Trust incoming X-Request-Id header from proxy.

items.name: logger
items.description: Simple request-logging middleware. Logs method, url, status code, and response time.

items.name: errorHandler
items.description: Configurable error-handling middleware that formats error responses based on environment (dev vs production), supports custom formatters, and integrates with HttpError classes.

options.notes: Include stack traces in responses (default: true when NODE_ENV !== 'production').
Custom response formatter: (err, req, isDev) => object.
Callback on every error: (err, req, res) => void.

returns.description: Error-handling middleware `(err, req, res, next) => void`.
